text:
It was the year before I started high school. Every weekend morning, I’d wake up early and head to the local library. There I would find my favorite bookkeeper, a lady with white hair and glasses that adjusted strangely at every angle. She’d tell old tales of brave knights and daring dragons. Those tales got me through the lonely afternoons when I’d hang out there after my mom dropped me off.

One summer, I discovered my true passion wasn’t tales of chivalry—but the tales of heroes who didn’t want anything—they just happened.

That summer, I read about a dragon. Not just any dragon; this one wasn’t evil. Oh no. It was wise. And it would show you the world you’d never see. It was called Sir Fyldenwing because it had such gentle wings.

I imagined walking through mountains so tall I could barely see over them. I dreamed of caves so deep they’d swallow you whole. I dreamed of clouds that spark
